message:
feat(ci): add dedicated PTY tests workflow with retry logic

- Create .github/workflows/pty-tests.yml for PTY E2E tests
- Add retry logic (2 attempts) for flaky PTY tests
- Run tests with `script` command for PTY allocation
- Capture and upload test artifacts (logs, e2e outputs)
- Non-blocking CI (continue-on-error: true)
- Detailed GitHub job summary with test results
- Sync beads: close bd-1jhf, bd-1gff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ei","title":"demo_showcase spec: content pack (sample code, logs, links, unicode strings, metrics)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Content Pack

## Purpose

The demo needs a high-quality “content pack” so it looks like a real application and also proves correctness:

- realistic code to edit (highlighting + editing)
- logs that look like real systems output
- deterministic metrics for charts
- Unicode strings that prove grapheme + width correctness
- hyperlink URLs to prove OSC 8 integration

This bead defines the canonical content sets so every other bead can reference them without re-inventing strings.

## Content Sets

### 1) Editor Sample Content (highlighting + editing)

Primary buffer: a small Rust module that contains:

- `struct`/`enum` + `impl`
- lifetimes + generics
- doc comments + line comments
- `match`, `if let`, `Result`/`?`
- strings with escapes + raw strings
- a few `TODO:` style comments (to look realistic)

Secondary buffer (optional): Markdown with fenced Rust code blocks to prove tokenizer switching (if supported by the built-in registries).

**Important constraint:** keep the editor sample mostly to single-codepoint characters. Avoid multi-codepoint graphemes (ZWJ emoji, combining sequences) in the editor text itself, because `EditorView::render_to` does not use the grapheme pool path.

### 2) Logs Content (event stream)

The logs panel should show an event stream with:

- timestamps
- `INFO/WARN/ERROR` levels
- subsystem tags (`renderer`, `input`, `preview`, `tour`, etc.)
- a few “actionable” entries that are linkified via OSC 8

Example (conceptual):

- `[22:05:12] INFO  renderer: diff=124 cells, bytes=3.1KB`
- `[22:05:13] WARN  input: focus lost (paused)`
- `[22:05:14] ERROR preview: simulated failure (press R to retry)`

### 3) Metrics Content (charts + animation)

Metrics must be deterministic (derived from time + fixed parameters):

- FPS + frame time
- “CPU usage” synthetic waveform
- “memory bytes” synthetic counter
- a periodic “pulse” value for glow animations

No randomness (so tour mode is reproducible and tests can assert on values).

### 4) Unicode / Width Prove‑Out Strings (pool-rendered)

These strings must be rendered using the grapheme pool path (`buffer_with_pool` + `draw_text_with_pool` / `render_to_with_pool`) so correctness is visible.

Include at least:

- CJK wide chars: `漢字かなカナ`
- single-codepoint emoji: `🎉👍😀`
- multi-codepoint ZWJ emoji: `👨‍👩‍👧` / `👩‍💻` / `🧑‍🚀`
- combining marks: `a\u{0301}` (á) and `n\u{0303}` (ñ)

Also include a fixed “width ruler” line under each string so a viewer can see alignment and width decisions.

### 5) Hyperlink URLs (OSC 8)

Use stable URLs that are obviously legitimate:

- `https://github.com/anomalyco/opentui`
- `https://github.com/anomalyco/opentui/tree/main/src`
- `https://unicode.org/reports/tr11/`

The exact URLs are less important than:

- they’re real
- they’re visually recognizable
- at least one is rendered in a log row and in a “docs”/help context

## Why This Matters

A demo’s credibility lives or dies on its content. If the strings are artificial or the logs look fake, the viewer won’t trust the engine.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:

## Purpose

Implement a scrollable sidebar that proves:

- nested scissor clipping
- scroll offsets
- mouse wheel scrolling
- click hit testing per row

…and that drives the app’s **Section** navigation coherently.

## Content

Sidebar items (example):

- Overview
- Editor
- Preview
- Logs
- Unicode
- Performance
- Settings

## Behavior

- Up/Down selects item (when focused)
- Mouse wheel scrolls list (when hovered)
- Click selects item and focuses sidebar

Navigation semantics:

- Selecting an item updates `App.section` (see state machine bead).
- Navigating to a section also sets a sensible default focus:
  - Editor -> focus editor
  - Preview/Unicode/Performance -> focus preview
  - Logs -> focus logs

In compact layout, `section` determines which panel is shown full-screen.

## Rendering

- Panel border (focused highlights)
- Header “NAV”
- Scrollbar indicator
- Selected row uses selection background (alpha overlay)

## HitGrid

- Register a hit area per visible row.

-{"id":"bd-1gff","title":"Subtask: PTY CI Infrastructure","description":"# PTY CI Infrastructure

## Scope
Set up CI to run PTY tests reliably.

## Requirements

### Runner Configuration
- Ubuntu runner (PTY support)
- Allocate PTY before tests
- Set TERM environment variable
- Install expect if needed

### Job Configuration
\`\`\`yaml
pty-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 15
  env:
    TERM: xterm-256color
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Setup Rust
      uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
      
    - name: Build
      run: cargo build --release --bin demo_showcase
      
    - name: Run PTY tests
      run: |
        # Allocate PTY for test process
        script -q -c "cargo test --test pty_e2e -- --ignored --nocapture" /dev/null
        
    - name: Upload logs
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
      with:
        name: pty-logs
        path: target/pty-test-logs/
\`\`\`

### Artifact Capture
- PTY session logs
- Test output
- Timing information
- Failure screenshots (if possible)

### Retry Configuration
- Allow 1 retry for flaky tests
- Separate job for PTY tests (doesn't block main CI)

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/pty-tests.yml

- PTY tests run in CI
- Artifacts captured
- Flakiness < 5%

 {"id":"bd-1gy","title":"demo_showcase spec: deterministic tour script (auto-demo steps)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Deterministic Tour Script

## Why Tour Mode Exists

The showcase must be *demoable without a human driver*.

Tour mode provides:

- a deterministic sequence for recording videos
- a “guided explanation” overlay for first-time users
- an automatic prove-out of advanced features

## Tour Model

- Tour is a sequence of steps: `Step { title, description, duration_ms, action }`
- Each step can:
  - mutate app state (select panel, scroll, open overlay, type text)
  - trigger an animation or highlight effect
  - pop a toast describing what’s happening
- Determinism:
  - fixed durations
  - seeded animation parameters (no randomness; if “random”, derive from step index)

## Proposed Steps (Feature-Proving Sequence)

1. **Welcome + No Flicker**
   - Show title + smooth animated accent bar.
   - Toast: “Diff rendering + synchronized output (if supported).”

2. **Scissor-Clipped Sidebar Scroll**
   - Auto-scroll sidebar list inside clip rect.
   - Visual: scroll indicator.

3. **Focus + Hit Testing**
   - Simulate “clicking” top bar buttons (help/palette) and switching panel focus.
   - Visual: border glow moves.

4. **Command Palette (Glass Overlay)**
   - Open palette overlay.
   - Demonstrate opacity stack + blended background.

5. **Editor: Typing + Undo/Redo**
   - Insert a few lines.
   - Undo/redo quickly.
   - Toast: “Rope + undo groups.”

6. **Editor: Highlighting + Theme Switch**
   - Turn on syntax highlighting.
   - Switch theme (Dark → Light → High contrast).

7. **Bracketed Paste Proof**
   - Inject a synthetic Paste event into editor.
   - Toast: “Bracketed paste parsed and inserted.”

8. **Unicode + Width + Grapheme Pool**
   - Render a line containing:
     - CJK wide chars
     - single-codepoint emoji
     - multi-codepoint ZWJ emoji (must require pool)
   - Show a small “width ruler” under it.

9. **Preview: PixelBuffer + Supersampling**
   - Animate a small pixel-art + sparkline chart.

10. **Alpha Blending / Opacity**
   - Draw a translucent “modal” over preview with a drop-shadow-ish effect.

11. **Hyperlinks (OSC 8)**
   - Render clickable links (docs/GitHub) in the logs panel.
   - Visual: link styling + hover/pressed feedback.

12. **Finale: Performance Overlay**
   - Show render stats (fps, changed cells, bytes estimate).
   - End with a “you can build this” message.

## User Controls

- Start/stop: `Ctrl+T`
- Next: `Enter` / `Space`
- Prev: `Backspace`
- Exit tour: `Esc`

Unattended runs:

- When started with `--exit-after-tour`, the final tour step completion triggers a clean process exit (useful for recordings and PTY E2E).

## Acceptance Style

Tour steps should include micro-copy that explains *why* each feature matters.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Step list covers all advanced features (no gaps)
- [ ] Each step has a deterministic duration + action
- [ ] Tour can run unattended start-to-finish
- [ ] Tour UI explains what’s being demonstrated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:37.649964906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:17.441750420Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:17.441604188Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:07.758580540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-1ok","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:09.880209710Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:37.672251398Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:03.007134873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:05.596193414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1j0.8","title":"Benchmark: Input parsing operations","description":"# Benchmark: Input Parsing Operations

## Purpose

Benchmark the input event parsing subsystem. Fast input parsing is critical for responsive terminal applications.

## Benchmarks to Implement

### Key Event Parsing

```rust
fn bench_key_event_parsing(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("input_parsing");
    
    // Common key sequences
    let key_sequences = [
        (b"a".to_vec(), "single_char"),
        (b"\x1b[A".to_vec(), "arrow_up"),
        (b"\x1b[1;5C".to_vec(), "ctrl_right"),
        (b"\x1bOP".to_vec(), "f1"),
        (b"\x1b[15~".to_vec(), "f5"),
        (b"\x1b[200~".to_vec(), "bracketed_paste_start"),
    ];
    
    for (seq, name) in key_sequences {
        group.bench_function(name, |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                parse_key_event(black_box(&seq))
            })
        });
    }
    group.finish();
}
```

### Mouse Event Parsing

```rust
fn bench_mouse_event_parsing(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("mouse_parsing");
    
    // SGR mouse sequences
    let mouse_sequences = [
        (b"\x1b[<0;10;20M".to_vec(), "button_press"),
        (b"\x1b[<0;10;20m".to_vec(), "button_release"),
        (b"\x1b[<32;10;20M".to_vec(), "mouse_move"),
        (b"\x1b[<64;10;20M".to_vec(), "scroll_up"),
    ];
    
    for (seq, name) in mouse_sequences {
        group.bench_function(name, |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                parse_mouse_event(black_box(&seq))
            })
        });
    }
    group.finish();
}
```

### Escape Sequence Classification

```rust
fn bench_escape_classification(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark the initial escape sequence classifier
    c.bench_function("classify_escape_sequence", |b| {
        let sequences = [
            b"\x1b[A".to_vec(),
            b"\x1bOP".to_vec(),
            b"\x1b[<0;1;1M".to_vec(),
            b"\x1b]0;title\x07".to_vec(),
        ];
        
        b.iter(|| {
            for seq in &sequences {
                black_box(classify_escape_sequence(seq));
            }
        })
    });
}
```

### Throughput Benchmark

```rust
fn bench_input_throughput(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Simulate rapid typing/mouse movement
    let mut input_stream = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..1000 {
        input_stream.extend_from_slice(b"a");
        input_stream.extend_from_slice(b"\x1b[<32;10;20M");
    }
    
    c.bench_function("input_throughput_1000_events", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            let mut events = Vec::new();
            let mut parser = InputParser::new();
            parser.parse_all(black_box(&input_stream), &mut events)
        })
    });
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify

- CREATE: `benches/input.rs`
- MODIFY: `benches/mod.rs` (if exists)

## Expected Performance

Document expected ranges:
- Single key parse: <500ns
- Mouse event parse: <1μs
- 1000 events: <1ms

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All input parsing benchmarks implemented
- [ ] Performance documented
- [ ] No panics on malformed input
- [ ] Throughput benchmark included
- [ ] Results added to performance docs

 {"id":"bd-1j0.9","title":"Benchmark: Syntax highlighting operations","description":"# Benchmark: Syntax Highlighting Operations

## Purpose

Benchmark the syntax highlighting system to ensure it meets AGENTS.md performance requirements. This is CRITICAL because highlighting runs on every text change.

## Performance Targets (from AGENTS.md principles)

| Operation | Target | Rationale |
|-----------|--------|-----------|
| Single line tokenize | <1μs | Zero perceived latency |
| Incremental update (1 line change) | <1ms | Smooth typing |
| Full highlight (1000 lines) | <50ms | Acceptable file open time |
| Full highlight (10000 lines) | <500ms | Large file tolerance |
| Theme switch | <100μs | Instant visual feedback |

## Benchmarks to Implement

### 1. Tokenizer Benchmarks

```rust
fn bench_tokenize_single_line(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let tokenizer = RustTokenizer::new();
    let lines = [
        "fn main() { println!(\"hello\"); }",
        "let x: HashMap<String, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();",
        "/// This is a doc comment with `code`",
        "#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]",
    ];
    
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("tokenize_line");
    for (i, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {
        group.bench_function(format!("line_{i}"), |b| {
            b.iter(|| tokenizer.tokenize_line(black_box(line), LineState::Normal))
        });
    }
    group.finish();
}

fn bench_tokenize_file(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let tokenizer = RustTokenizer::new();
    let source = include_str!("../src/lib.rs");  // Real Rust file
    
    c.bench_function("tokenize_full_file", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            let mut state = LineState::Normal;
            for line in source.lines() {
                let (_, new_state) = tokenizer.tokenize_line(line, state);
                state = new_state;
            }
        })
    });
}
```

```rust
fn bench_incremental_single_line(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let source = include_str!("../src/lib.rs");
    let buffer = TextBuffer::from_str(source);
    let mut highlighted = HighlightedBuffer::new(buffer)
        .with_tokenizer(Box::new(RustTokenizer::new()))
        .with_theme(Theme::dark());
    highlighted.update_highlighting();  // Initial full highlight
    
    c.bench_function("incremental_single_line", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            highlighted.mark_dirty(100);  // Mark line 100 dirty
```

## Properties to Test

### Grapheme Iteration
```
∀ s ∈ valid UTF-8 strings:
  graphemes = s.graphemes(true).collect()
  - graphemes.join("") == s  # lossless
  - graphemes.iter().all(|g| g.len() > 0)  # no empty graphemes
```

### Display Width Consistency
```
∀ s ∈ valid UTF-8 strings:
  width1 = display_width(s)
  width2 = display_width(s)
  - width1 == width2  # deterministic
  - width >= 0
  - width <= s.chars().count() * 2  # max 2 per char
```

### Line Wrapping
```
∀ text, max_width where max_width > 0:
  wrapped = wrap_text(text, max_width)
  - wrapped.iter().all(|line| display_width(line) <= max_width)
  - wrapped content equals original (minus added breaks)
```

### Word Boundaries
```
∀ s ∈ valid UTF-8 strings:
  boundaries = word_boundaries(s)
  - boundaries are sorted
  - boundaries[0] == 0 or boundaries is empty
  - boundaries.last() <= s.len()
```

 {"id":"bd-1m9","title":"demo_showcase runtime: non-blocking input pump (stdin read + InputParser + event queue)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Non-Blocking Input Pump

## Purpose

Build a robust input pipeline:

- Read bytes from stdin without blocking the render loop
- Parse using `opentui::InputParser`
- Queue structured `Event`s for dispatch
- Support both “real input” and synthetic events (tour mode)

## Recommended Approach

Reuse the known-good pattern from `examples/editor.rs`:

- Use `libc::select` (or `poll`) to read with a timeout
- Maintain an `input_accumulator: Vec<u8>`
- Repeatedly call `parser.parse(&input_accumulator[offset..])` until:
  - `ParseError::Incomplete` (need more bytes)
  - `ParseError::Empty`

## Edge Cases

- Partial escape sequences spanning reads
- Bracketed paste payloads (large) → ensure accumulator is bounded
- Unknown bytes → skip 1 byte and continue

A function/struct something like:

```rust
    scratch: [u8; 1024],
}

}
```

And a path to inject synthetic events for tour mode:

```rust

```

 {"id":"bd-1m90","title":"Proptest: Buffer diff algorithm property tests","description":"# Property Tests: Buffer Diff Algorithm

## Properties to Test

### Diff Correctness
```
∀ buf1, buf2 ∈ OptimizedBuffer (same dimensions):
  diff = BufferDiff::compute(buf1, buf2)
  - applying diff to buf1 produces buf2
  - diff.changed_cells contains all different cells
  - diff.changed_cells contains no identical cells
```

### Diff Minimality
```
∀ buf1, buf2:
  diff = BufferDiff::compute(buf1, buf2)
  - diff.change_count <= width * height
  - if buf1 == buf2: diff.change_count == 0
```

### Region Merging
```
∀ diff with changes:
  - dirty_regions cover all changed_cells
  - regions don't unnecessarily overlap
  - total region area >= change_count
```

### Bits Equality Consistency
```
∀ cell1, cell2 ∈ Cell:
  - cell1.bits_eq(&cell2) ⟺ (cell1 visually identical to cell2)
  - bits_eq is symmetric: a.bits_eq(b) == b.bits_eq(a)
  - bits_eq is reflexive: a.bits_eq(a) == true
```

 {"id":"bd-1ms","title":"Add Word Movement and Deletion Methods","description":"## Overview
Add cursor movement and deletion methods that use existing word boundary detection.

## Already Implemented (NO WORK NEEDED)
The following methods already exist in `src/text/edit.rs`:
- `get_next_word_boundary()` at line 397 ✓
- `get_prev_word_boundary()` at line 418 ✓

## Scope (Methods to Add)
```rust
impl EditBuffer {
    /// Move cursor to next word (Ctrl+Right behavior).
    pub fn move_word_right(&mut self) {
        let boundary = self.get_next_word_boundary();
        self.move_to_offset(boundary);
    }

    /// Move cursor to previous word (Ctrl+Left behavior).
    pub fn move_word_left(&mut self) {
        let boundary = self.get_prev_word_boundary();
        self.move_to_offset(boundary);
    }

    /// Delete from cursor to next word boundary (Ctrl+Delete behavior).
    pub fn delete_word_forward(&mut self) {
        let end = self.get_next_word_boundary();
        if end > self.cursor.offset {
            self.delete_range_offsets(self.cursor.offset, end);
        }
    }

    /// Delete from previous word boundary to cursor (Ctrl+Backspace behavior).
    pub fn delete_word_backward(&mut self) {
        let start = self.get_prev_word_boundary();
        if start < self.cursor.offset {
            self.delete_range_offsets(start, self.cursor.offset);
        }
    }
}
```

- src/text/edit.rs - Add the 4 new methods

```rust
fn test_move_word_right() {
    let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("hello world test");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", buffer.text());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Initial cursor: offset={}", buffer.cursor().offset);
    
    buffer.move_word_right();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After move_word_right: offset={}", buffer.cursor().offset);
    assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().offset, 5, "Should be at end of 'hello'");
    
    buffer.move_word_right();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After second move_word_right: offset={}", buffer.cursor().offset);
    assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().offset, 11, "Should be at end of 'world'");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: move_word_right works");
}

#[test]

    let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("hello world test");
    buffer.move_to_end();  // Move to end
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", buffer.text());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Cursor at end: offset={}", buffer.cursor().offset);
    
    buffer.move_word_left();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After move_word_left: offset={}", buffer.cursor().offset);
    assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().offset, 12, "Should be at start of 'test'");
    
    buffer.move_word_left();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After second move_word_left: offset={}", buffer.cursor().offset);
    assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().offset, 6, "Should be at start of 'world'");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: move_word_left works");

#[test]
fn test_delete_word_forward() {
    let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("hello world test");
    buffer.move_to_offset(6);  // Start of "world"
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", buffer.text());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Cursor at offset 6 (start of 'world')");

    eprintln!("[TEST]   Text: {:?}", buffer.text());

    assert_eq!(buffer.text(), "hello  test", "Should delete 'world'");

#[test]
fn test_word_operations_across_lines() {
    let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("line one\nline two");
    buffer.move_to_offset(9);  // Start of "line two"
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text with newline: {:?}", buffer.text());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Cursor at offset 9");
    
    buffer.move_word_left();
    let cursor = buffer.cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After move_word_left: offset={} row={} col={}", 
        cursor.offset, cursor.row, cursor.col);
    
    assert!(cursor.offset < 9, "Should move to previous line");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Word operations cross line boundaries");
}

#[test]
fn test_word_operations_at_boundaries() {
    let mut buffer = EditBuffer::with_text("hello");
    
    // At start - move left should stay at 0
    buffer.move_word_left();
    assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().offset, 0);
    eprintln!("[TEST] move_word_left at start stays at 0");
    
    // At end - move right should stay at end
    buffer.move_to_end();
    let end = buffer.cursor().offset;
    buffer.move_word_right();
    assert_eq!(buffer.cursor().offset, end);
    eprintln!("[TEST] move_word_right at end stays at end");
    
    // Delete at boundaries - should be no-op
    buffer.move_to_end();
    buffer.delete_word_forward();
    assert_eq!(buffer.text(), "hello", "delete_word_forward at end is no-op");
    
    buffer.move_to_offset(0);
    buffer.delete_word_backward();
    assert_eq!(buffer.text(), "hello", "delete_word_backward at start is no-op");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Word operations handle boundaries gracefully");
```

## Scope
Test complete input handling from raw bytes → parsed events → application response, with comprehensive simulation utilities for complex workflows.

## Test Infrastructure

### InputSequence Builder
```rust
pub struct InputSequence {
    events: Vec<InputEvent>,
    timing: TimingMode, // Instant, Realistic, Stress
}

impl InputSequence {
    pub fn type_text(s: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn keystroke(key: KeyCode, mods: Modifiers) -> Self;
    pub fn mouse_click(x: u16, y: u16, button: MouseButton) -> Self;
    pub fn mouse_drag(from: (u16,u16), to: (u16,u16)) -> Self;
    pub fn paste(content: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn with_delay(self, ms: u64) -> Self;
}
```

### Timing Modes
- **Instant**: All events at once (stress testing)
- **Realistic**: 50-200 WPM typing speed simulation
- **Stress**: Rapid burst input

## Test Scenarios

### Keyboard Input Flow (6 tests)
- [ ] Single keypress → KeyEvent
- [ ] Modifier combinations (Ctrl+X, Alt+Y, Ctrl+Alt+Z)
- [ ] Function keys (F1-F12)
- [ ] Special keys (arrows, home, end, pgup, pgdn, insert, delete)
- [ ] Unicode character input (multi-byte UTF-8)
- [ ] Escape key handling (vs Alt prefix)

### Mouse Input Flow (5 tests)
- [ ] Click → MouseEvent with correct position
- [ ] Drag sequence (press → move → release)
- [ ] Scroll wheel events (up/down)
- [ ] Mouse position accuracy at boundaries
- [ ] Mouse button combinations (left+right)

### Paste Input (4 tests)
- [ ] Bracketed paste detection (\x1b[200~ ... \x1b[201~)
- [ ] Large paste handling (>10KB)
- [ ] Binary data in paste (no corruption)
- [ ] Paste with special characters and Unicode

### Event to Render Response (4 tests)
- [ ] Keypress → buffer change → present
- [ ] Mouse click → hit test → response render
- [ ] Resize event → buffer resize → present
- [ ] Focus change → application state update

### Complex Workflows (6 tests)
- [ ] Open file → edit → save simulation
- [ ] Navigate menu → select option → confirm
- [ ] Scroll → click → drag selection
- [ ] Search → navigate results → select
- [ ] Undo → redo → undo sequence
- [ ] Multi-window focus switching

### Rapid Input (3 tests)
- [ ] Typing speed simulation (50-200 WPM)
- [ ] Burst input (100+ keys/second)
- [ ] Input during rendering (race condition check)

### Edge Cases (4 tests)
- [ ] Partial escape sequences (interrupted input)
- [ ] Invalid/malformed sequences (graceful handling)
- [ ] Timeout handling (escape vs alt)
- [ ] Input queue overflow behavior

## Logging Points
```jsonl

{"step": "parse", "event": {"type": "key", "code": "A", "modifiers": []}}
{"step": "handle", "action": "insert_char", "char": "A"}
{"step": "render", "changed_cells": 1, "frame": 42}
```

## Verification
- Verify buffer state after each sequence
- Verify cursor position
- Verify selection state
- Timing assertions for performance
- No dropped events under stress

## Files to Create
- tests/common/input_sim.rs - InputSequence implementation
- tests/e2e_input_flow.rs - input flow tests
- tests/e2e_workflows.rs - complex workflow tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- 30+ input/workflow test scenarios
- Rapid input stress tests pass
- Mouse drag operations tested
- Paste edge cases covered
- All keyboard/mouse flows verified
- No dropped events under stress
- JSONL logging for all events","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:46:21.439253421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:03.750487024Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nfp","depends_on_id":"bd-yps6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:46:21.446096669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ob","title":"demo_showcase runtime: resilience + capability gating implementation","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Resilience + Capability Gating Implementation

## Purpose

The demo must look great on ideal terminals, but it must also be robust when:

- terminal is too small
- true color is not supported
- hyperlinks are unsupported
- mouse events aren’t available
- stdout/stderr aren’t TTYs

This bead implements the degradation rules defined in `bd-1i7`.

## Behaviors

### Small Terminal

- Define a minimum supported size (from spec).
- Below that:
  - switch to a compact layout (single column or stacked panels)
  - reduce visual density (fewer borders, smaller headers)
  - ensure no panics / out-of-bounds due to rect math

### Missing Capabilities

- If no truecolor: use a reduced palette (256-color-safe or basic).
- If no hyperlinks: render link text without OSC 8 and add a `(copy)` hint.
- If no mouse: hide click hints, ensure keyboard-only navigation covers everything.
- If focus events not available: don’t depend on them for pause behavior.

### Non‑TTY

- Provide a safe fallback:
  - `--headless-smoke` should work without a TTY.
  - If run interactively without TTY and not headless, print a one-line guidance message and exit with non-zero.

## Capability Override (Testability)

To make resilience testable and debuggable, the demo supports an optional capability preset override:

- `--cap-preset <name>` (see `bd-2iv`)

Rules:

- Interactive (TTY): presets can only DISABLE capabilities (effective = detected ∩ preset).
- Headless: presets define the effective caps directly.

The UI should surface effective capabilities (inspector + status hints) so the viewer understands why a feature is disabled.

This is exercised by the degradation matrix suite (`bd-2bnv`).

## Acceptance Notes

- The demo never panics due to resize or missing terminal features.
- UI clearly indicates when features are disabled (subtle warning in status/inspector).
- Degradation behavior is consistent and snapshottable in headless mode.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- The demo never panics due to missing capabilities or resize/relayout.
- Below minimum size, compact layout is activated and remains usable (help/quit always visible).
- When features are disabled (no mouse/links/truecolor/sync), the UI degrades gracefully and indicates the limitation.
- `--cap-preset` only disables capabilities in interactive mode (effective = detected ∩ preset) and defines caps in headless mode.
- The degradation matrix suite (`bd-2bnv`) passes and provides ongoing regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:49.434807357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:34:31.497181251Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:34:31.497040558Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:10.635614919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-1xsw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:16.340490585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:49.450381440Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:11.169561512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-3ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:11.747840355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-ix0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:12.288427025Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-pnln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:18.077506237Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ok","title":"demo_showcase spec: visual design system (palette, typography, spacing, components)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Visual Design System

## Goal

A consistent, modern visual language that makes OpenTUI feel premium:

- strong spacing rhythm
- crisp borders
- tasteful gradients
- glassy overlays
- clear focus/selection

This spec defines **multiple themes** because the demo explicitly showcases theme switching (UI + highlighting) as a real app capability.

## Theme Set

### Theme A: “Synthwave Professional” (Default, Dark)

Base background:
- BG0 = #0f1220 (app background)
- BG1 = #151a2e (panel background)
- BG2 = #1d2440 (raised surfaces / cards)

Text:
- FG0 = #e6e6e6 (primary)
- FG1 = #aeb6d6 (secondary)
- FG2 = #6c7396 (dim)

Accents:
- Aqua   = #4dd6ff
- Pink   = #ff4fd8
- Green  = #2bff88
- Orange = #ffb020
- Red    = #ff4455

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #2a335c (typically applied as a low-alpha overlay)
- focus border: Aqua (and bold)

### Theme B: “Light (Paper)”

Base background:
- BG0 = #f7f7fb
- BG1 = #ffffff
- BG2 = #eef0f7

Text:
- FG0 = #1a1b26
- FG1 = #3a3f5a
- FG2 = #6a6f8a

Accents:
- Blue   = #2a6fff
- Purple = #7b61ff
- Green  = #00a86b
- Orange = #ff8a00
- Red    = #e53935

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #dbe6ff (low alpha)
- focus border: Blue

### Theme C: “Solarized-ish” (Low Eye Strain)

Base background:
- BG0 = #002b36
- BG1 = #073642
- BG2 = #0b4452

Text:
- FG0 = #eee8d5
- FG1 = #93a1a1
- FG2 = #657b83

Accents:
- Cyan   = #2aa198
- Yellow = #b58900
- Orange = #cb4b16
- Red    = #dc322f
- Blue   = #268bd2

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #0d5161 (low alpha)
- focus border: Cyan

### Theme D: “High Contrast” (Accessibility / Worst-Case Terminals)

Base background:
- BG0 = #000000
- BG1 = #000000
- BG2 = #111111

Text:
- FG0 = #ffffff
- FG1 = #e0e0e0
- FG2 = #a0a0a0

Accents:
- Cyan   = #00ffff
- Yellow = #ffff00
- Magenta= #ff00ff
- Green  = #00ff00
- Red    = #ff0000

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #333333
- focus border: Yellow

## Component Styling Tokens (Theme-Relative)

- Panel border: dim FG2
- Focused panel border: focus accent + bold + optional 1px “glow line”
- Headers: gradient bar + bold title
- Status bar: BG2 with muted text; hotkeys in FG0 bold
- Toasts: rounded box + opacity stack (glass)

## Typography / Characters

- Prefer Unicode box drawing (rounded/heavy where appropriate).
- Use subtle separators: │, ─, ·.
- Keep iconography mostly to single-codepoint symbols for stable width.

## Gradient Rules

- Gradients should be subtle: 5–10% perceived change across width.
- Use Rgba::lerp between two close hues.

## Theme Switching Rules

- Theme switching changes BOTH:
  1) UI palette/token set
  2) syntax highlighting theme (Theme::dark/light/…)

The demo should present theme switching as a real workflow feature:

- via command palette
- optionally via number keys when help is open (discoverable)

## Degradation Rules (Terminal Capability Variance)

- If no truecolor, map theme colors to the closest 256-color approximation.
- Avoid relying on italics/strikethrough; treat as “bonus” if supported.

## What This Bead Produces

- Canonical theme token sets used everywhere in the demo.
- A single “current theme” selection mechanism shared by chrome, panels, overlays, toasts, and highlighting.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette tokens defined (BG/FG/Accents)
- [ ] Focus/selection styling rules defined
- [ ] Component styling rules defined (borders, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient rules defined
- [ ] Degradation rules defined","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.420443553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358210255Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358144072Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created docs/DEMO_SHOWCASE_DESIGN_SYSTEM.md with 4 theme definitions, component styling, typography, spacing, gradients, and degradation rules","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.450428345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.107964883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

The demo should boot into a believable “project workspace” with rich content immediately visible.

This bead wires the content pack spec (`bd-1ei`) into real runtime state:

- initial editor buffers
- initial sidebar navigation structure
- initial logs stream
- deterministic metrics sources

- `DemoContent` struct:
  - `files: Vec<DemoFile { name, language_hint, text }>`
  - `links: DemoLinks { repo_url, docs_url, ... }`
  - `seed_logs: Vec<LogEntry>`
  - `metric_params: MetricParams`

And a single initializer:

- `fn App::new(content: DemoContent, ...) -> App`

## Design Notes

- The editor’s initial file should be Rust and highlight well with the built-in tokenizer registry.
- The preview panel should have data available immediately (no empty state).
- Logs should start with a small backlog so scrolling is demonstrable.
- Metrics should come from deterministic functions (sin waves, ramps) so tour mode is reproducible.

 {"id":"bd-ix0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: resize handling (SIGWINCH/crossterm-style events) + safe relayout","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Resize Handling + Safe Relayout

## Purpose

The demo must survive resizes without flicker or panics.

## Inputs

- Primary: `Event::Resize(ResizeEvent { width, height })` from `InputParser`
- Secondary fallback: periodic `terminal_size()` polling (optional) if resize events are missing

## Behavior

On resize:

1. Call `renderer.resize(new_w, new_h)`
2. Recompute layout rectangles
3. Reset scroll positions if out of bounds
4. Push a toast: `Resized to WxH`

## Pitfalls

- Ensure we never compute negative/overflowing rects.
- Ensure compact-mode triggers below minimum sizes.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:

 {"id":"bd-j7g","title":"Terminal Capability Queries","description":"## Overview
Send actual capability queries to the terminal instead of just checking environment variables. This enables accurate feature detection for advanced terminal features.

## Scope
Query sequences to send (per spec Section 2.7):
- DA1 (Primary Device Attributes): `ESC[c`
- DA2 (Secondary Device Attributes): `ESC[>c`
- XTVERSION: `ESC[>0q`
- Pixel resolution: `ESC[14t`
- Kitty keyboard protocol: `ESC[?u`

Parse responses to detect:
- Terminal type and version
- True color support
- Kitty keyboard protocol support
- Synchronized output support
- Sixel graphics support
- SGR pixel mouse mode

## Implementation Details

### Query Sequences
```rust
// In src/terminal/queries.rs
pub const DA1_QUERY: &str = "\x1b[c";           // Primary device attributes
pub const DA2_QUERY: &str = "\x1b[>c";          // Secondary device attributes
pub const XTVERSION_QUERY: &str = "\x1b[>0q";   // Terminal version
pub const PIXEL_SIZE_QUERY: &str = "\x1b[14t";  // Window pixel size
pub const KITTY_KEYBOARD_QUERY: &str = "\x1b[?u"; // Kitty keyboard support
```

### Response Parser
```rust
impl Terminal {
    /// Send capability queries and collect responses.
    /// Uses timeout to avoid blocking if terminal doesn't respond.
    pub fn query_capabilities(&mut self, timeout: Duration) -> io::Result<()>;

    /// Parse a terminal response sequence.
    fn parse_response(&mut self, response: &[u8]) -> Option<TerminalResponse>;

    /// Check for pending responses (non-blocking).
    pub fn poll_responses(&mut self) -> Vec<TerminalResponse>;
}

pub enum TerminalResponse {
    DeviceAttributes { 
        primary: bool,  // DA1 vs DA2
        params: Vec<u32>,
    },
    XtVersion { 
        name: String, 
        version: String,
    },
    PixelSize { 
        width: u16, 
        height: u16,
    },
    KittyKeyboard { 
        flags: u32,
    },
    Unknown(Vec<u8>),
}
```

### Response Format Parsing
```
DA1 response: ESC [ ? Ps ; Ps ; ... c
  - Parse params to detect features (e.g., 4 = sixel)
  
DA2 response: ESC [ > Pp ; Pv ; Pc c
  - Pp = terminal type, Pv = firmware version, Pc = ROM cartridge
  
XTVERSION response: ESC P > | text ST
  - text contains terminal name and version
  
Pixel size response: ESC [ 4 ; height ; width t
  - Window size in pixels

Kitty keyboard response: ESC [ ? flags u
  - flags indicates supported modes
```

### Capability Updates
Map responses to Capabilities struct updates:
- DA1 param 4 → sixel = true
- XTVERSION contains "kitty" → kitty_keyboard = true, kitty_graphics = true
- XTVERSION contains "foot"/"alacritty"/"wezterm" → rgb = true
- Pixel size response → store for sixel/image calculations

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/terminal/queries.rs - new file for query constants and response types (~100 lines)
- src/terminal/mod.rs - add query methods and response handling (~150 lines)
- src/terminal/capabilities.rs - update from parsed responses (~50 lines)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (minimum 10 tests)
```rust
// Response parsing tests
#[test] fn test_parse_da1_response_basic()
#[test] fn test_parse_da1_response_with_sixel()
#[test] fn test_parse_da2_response()
#[test] fn test_parse_xtversion_kitty()
#[test] fn test_parse_xtversion_alacritty()
#[test] fn test_parse_pixel_size_response()
#[test] fn test_parse_kitty_keyboard_response()
#[test] fn test_parse_unknown_response()

// Query generation tests
#[test] fn test_query_sequences_correct()
#[test] fn test_query_all_sends_all_queries()

// Integration tests
#[test] fn test_capabilities_updated_from_da1()
#[test] fn test_timeout_returns_partial_results()
```

### E2E Test (tests/e2e/capability_queries.rs)
```rust
/// Interactive test that queries real terminal and logs results.
/// Run with: cargo test --test capability_e2e -- --nocapture
#[test]
fn e2e_query_real_terminal() {
    // Setup logging
    env_logger::init();
    
    let mut terminal = Terminal::new()?;
    
    log::info!("Sending capability queries...");
    terminal.query_capabilities(Duration::from_secs(2))?;
    
    for response in terminal.poll_responses() {
        log::info!("Response: {:?}", response);
    }
    
    log::info!("Final capabilities: {:?}", terminal.capabilities());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 query sequences sent in correct format
- [ ] DA1 response parsed correctly, sixel detected
- [ ] DA2 response parsed correctly
- [ ] XTVERSION response extracts terminal name/version
- [ ] Pixel size response extracts dimensions
- [ ] Kitty keyboard response parsed
- [ ] Unknown responses don't crash (return Unknown variant)
- [ ] Timeout prevents hanging on non-responsive terminals
- [ ] Capabilities struct updated from parsed responses
- [ ] All 10+ unit tests pass

+name: PTY E2E Tests
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main, master]
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main, master]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+# This workflow runs PTY tests separately from main CI to avoid blocking.
+# PTY tests are inherently flaky due to timing sensitivities and are not
+# required to pass for PRs to merge.
+
+concurrency:
+  group: pty-tests-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
+  TERM: xterm-256color
+
+jobs:
+  pty-tests:
+    name: PTY E2E Tests
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 15
+    # Continue on error - these tests are informational, not blocking
+    continue-on-error: true
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        # Run twice to check for flakiness
+        run: [1]
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          key: pty-tests
+
+      - name: Build demo_showcase binary
+        run: cargo build --bin demo_showcase
+
+      - name: Create artifacts directory
+        run: mkdir -p target/pty-test-logs
+
+      - name: Run PTY E2E tests (with retry)
+        id: pty_test
+        run: |
+          # Attempt 1
+          echo "=== Attempt 1 ===" | tee -a target/pty-test-logs/run.log
+          if script -q -c "cargo test --test pty_e2e -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1" /dev/null 2>&1 | tee -a target/pty-test-logs/run.log; then
+            echo "PTY tests passed on attempt 1"
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          echo "Attempt 1 failed, retrying..." | tee -a target/pty-test-logs/run.log
+          sleep 2
+
+          # Attempt 2 (retry)
+          echo "=== Attempt 2 ===" | tee -a target/pty-test-logs/run.log
+          if script -q -c "cargo test --test pty_e2e -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1" /dev/null 2>&1 | tee -a target/pty-test-logs/run.log; then
+            echo "PTY tests passed on attempt 2 (after retry)"
+            echo "result=pass_retry" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          echo "PTY tests failed after 2 attempts" | tee -a target/pty-test-logs/run.log
+          echo "result=fail" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          exit 1
+        env:
+          TERM: xterm-256color
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR: target/e2e-artifacts
+          HARNESS_LOG_LEVEL: debug
+
+      - name: Generate test summary
+        if: always()
+        run: |
+          echo "## PTY E2E Test Results" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+
+          RESULT="${{ steps.pty_test.outputs.result }}"
+          case "$RESULT" in
+            pass)
+              echo "Result: **PASSED** on first attempt" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+              ;;
+            pass_retry)
+              echo "Result: **PASSED** (after retry)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+              echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+              echo "> Note: Test required retry, may indicate flakiness" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+              ;;
+            fail)
+              echo "Result: **FAILED** after 2 attempts" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+              ;;
+            *)
+              echo "Result: **UNKNOWN**" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+              ;;
+          esac
+
+          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "### Artifacts" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+
+          if [ -d "target/e2e-artifacts" ]; then
+            echo "E2E artifacts captured:" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            find target/e2e-artifacts -type f -name "*.json" -o -name "*.txt" | head -10 | while read f; do
+              echo "- \`$(basename $f)\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            done
+          fi
+
+          if [ -f "target/pty-test-logs/run.log" ]; then
+            echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo "### Test Output (last 30 lines)" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo '```' >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            tail -30 target/pty-test-logs/run.log >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo '```' >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          fi
+
+      - name: Upload PTY test logs
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: pty-test-logs-${{ matrix.run }}
+          path: |
+            target/pty-test-logs/
+          retention-days: 7
+
+      - name: Upload E2E artifacts
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: pty-e2e-artifacts-${{ matrix.run }}
+          path: |
+            target/e2e-artifacts/
+          retention-days: 7
+
+  # Summary job for overall status
+  pty-summary:
+    name: PTY Test Summary
+    needs: [pty-tests]
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    if: always()
+    steps:
+      - name: Check results
+        run: |
+          echo "## PTY Test Suite Summary" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+
+          # Note: PTY tests are informational, not blocking
+          if [[ "${{ needs.pty-tests.result }}" == "success" ]]; then
+            echo "All PTY tests passed." >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          else
+            echo "PTY tests had failures (non-blocking)." >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+            echo "> These tests are informational and do not block merges." >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          fi
